About
Disclaimer

Disclaimer

I Ching Reading Guide publishes cultural information, reflective tools, educational articles, product notes, and paid report options. The content is for reflection, journaling, study, and decision review only. It is not professional advice.

General information only

Articles, hexagram notes, calculators, readings, worksheets, checklists, product pages, and paid reports are provided for general education and personal reflection. They may help you organize a question, slow down before acting, compare patterns, or write a clearer journal note. They should not be treated as medical, legal, financial, psychological, safety, emergency, or other professional guidance.

No medical, legal, financial, or mental health advice

Do not use this site to diagnose symptoms, choose treatment, manage medication, handle legal disputes, make investments, decide taxes, respond to emergencies, or replace therapy or crisis support. If a matter involves health, self-harm, immediate danger, abuse, legal deadlines, large financial risk, or personal safety, contact qualified professionals or emergency services first. A symbolic reading cannot verify facts or protect you from real-world risk.

Reflection and journaling boundary

An I Ching reading on this site should be used as a reflection note. It can help you ask what needs attention, what timing may be wise, what assumption should be checked, or what practical next step feels responsible. It does not guarantee a future event, reveal another person's private thoughts, prove compatibility, promise money, or remove your responsibility for decisions.

Advertising, affiliate links, and paid reports

This site may include advertising, affiliate links, product notes, digital reports, or checkout options. Visitors should verify prices, product details, refund terms, delivery terms, suitability, and third-party policies before purchasing. A paid report adds structure and explanation to a reading, but it remains reflective content and should not be used as a professional service.

User responsibility

You are responsible for how you use the information. Compare any reading with observable facts, direct communication, and appropriate expert advice. For questions about this disclaimer, contact guan@shanyuegroup.com.

I Ching Reading Guide

A calm I Ching tool for hexagrams, changing lines, and reflective decisions.
